entry.title: Re: How to use the Maintain Business Role Groups functionality
entry.content:
@Adiyaraja_AK 

We are looking at this group from compliance/controls perspective - each of our business roles have a business function that is responsible for it's assignment to users (i.e. functional owner has to approve any request from user to be assigned this role).

From perspective of holding records on ownership and responsibility, using the Business Role Group helps us maintain a shortlist of ownership functions to group these. Admittedly this can be done within naming convention of the roles themselves, but in some occasions this ownership may change over time, so having the Business Role Group concept allows us to capture this variation over time with internal reorganisations of structures/departments etc.

The fact that the Business Role Group can also be shown on download of business roles also assists in preparing periodic recertification exercises for internal controls; when looking to merge user-level data with role assignment data; this helps to prepare listings of relevant/specific info to the business functions to validate

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content:
@GabrieleRendina 

In my setup (S/4 2023 FPS01, Cloud Identity and Joule in same subaccount) I had the same error.

Solution for me was in step 1.5 of the blog: 

Enter the trusted domain in Identity Provider > Applications & Resources > Tenant Settings > Customization > Trusted Domains:
With a fully-qualified host name (the same I used in Cloud Connector) I got the error; when I used a wildcard (*.<your domain>) it worked.

Best, Joerg

entry.title: Re: can you add custom field(available on MIRO) in FS10N...
entry.content:
To add non customizing allowed fields (not BKPF, BSEG tables for example) to the item ALV FI reports (such as FBL*N transactions) you have to (3446219 - Requirement to add custom fields in the reports FBL1N/FBL3N/FBL5N or FAGLL03)

  1. Append fields to structure  RFPOSXEXT
  2. Use BAdI FI_ITEMS_CH_DATA to fill those fields with Abap code (BTE 1650 seems nowadays obsolete, also it was called for each record, so worse performance)

entry.title: Re: How to find Cloud ALM global/subaccount cockpit URL f...
entry.content:
Hi,

In short, it is not possible to derive the location of your SAP Cloud ALM BTP Global account or Sub account based on the information available in your SAP Cloud ALM tenant. 

When SAP Cloud ALM is provisioned, a new Global account and Sub account is created. The person that triggered the provisioning is the only person added as an Admin for the Global account and Sub account. This means that only they can see the Global account or Sub account in the BTP cockpit, no-one else will be able to see it.

This topic and its possible solutions are documented in this KBA: 3123560 - Cannot access the SAP Cloud ALM Global Account or Sub account in SAP BTP cockpit

I suggest that you follow the instructions in the KBA to properly identify your next steps to getting access to the SAP Cloud ALM BTP accounts.

Thanks and regards,
John

entry.title: Crystal Reports Viewer in Visual Studio 2022 - Installed but Disabled
entry.content:
I have been a great fan of Crystal Reports since version 9. I still have that old copy and it worked seamlessly with VB 6.0. Today, I am seriously frustrated trying to get the developer edition working in Visual Studio 2022. I have wanted to 'cry' when every solution posted elsewhere fails to work. These are my steps:

Downloaded and installed as administrator Crystal Reports for Visual Studio SP36. I called in the project reference assemblies:

CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine

CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Shared

CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.ReportSource

CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Windows.Forms

After ensuring their setup, i was surprised to find Crystal Reports Viewer missing from the toolbox. Knowing how to add, I right-click on the toolbox add items and found the control under .net framework and confirmed ok. However, i get a message that the control is installed but disabled I guess owing to framework incompatibility or whatever. 

From the SAP website, SP36 should work with Visual 2022. I am running a project on .Net 8.0 framework. Any advise from the creators? I need this working ASAP.

entry.title: Maintain Same invoicing party for 2 vendor codes
entry.content:
We have below scenario :

There are 2 plants (A and B) in company code 1.

Company 2 will purchase material from both plants of company code 1 

Our Client wants to raise invoices against only 1 vendor code as the company code is same for both the supplying plants.

To satisfy the requirement,

1) can be create 2 vendor codes in company 2 for each plant in company 1 (For ex. Vendor A and Vendor B)

2) Create separate purchase orders for each plants.

3) however, in the purchase order of Vendor B invoicing party can be kept as vendor A so that for both the cases invoice will be raised with same vendor code.(Vendor A)

Please let us know if the above approach is correct and is there any impact on financial accounting due to above approach.

Thank you,

Sumant

entry.title: Re: Availability of Combined Orders/Collective order Func...
entry.content:
Dear @kaushikan ,

The combined order functionality (known from ECC DIMP-solution (=Discrete Industries and Mill Product)) is unfortunately not yet available in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. However, having combined order functionalities is one of the highest voted customer influence request within public cloud manufacturing. Please see the link below and upvote it as well to follow the latest status. It has been accepted in the meantime, but time will tell in which release SAP will effectively incorporate this into public cloud. Unfortunately, I don't see it on the SAP roadmap explorer yet.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/286145/?section=sectionComments

If you think collective orders could be a solution to your problem, then please find below link as this is already available at the moment:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/757b3e759f304d34a2bb3b99f94eec5d.html?locale=en-US&q=collective+orders

Otherwise you will have to investigate a custom-built solution on BTP or a workaround until we will get combined orders functionality in public cloud.

Hope this helps.

Kind regards,
Jens

entry.title: Re: SAP CDP cannot properly ingest CDC custom data array
entry.content:
Hello there, 

Custom array handling in the CDC to CDP connector is indeed not supported today. This is primarily because the schema on the Customer Data Cloud side does not allow to declare a field as an array, and all custom fields could technically either hold arrays or single values / objects.

As a result, the CDC to CDP connector only ever maps the first value of an array into CDP. We are currently investigating ways to solve this, but this is not officially on the roadmap at this stage.

Best.

Romain

entry.title: Re: Inventory Transfer Referenced Document not showing in...
entry.content:
Hi @kedalenechong ,

The document tree will show documents belonging to the same process i.e. Order to Cash, Procure to Payment, etc. So, a sequence like Sales Order > Delivery > AR Invoice > Incoming Payment will be there. But, if you have created an Inventory Transfer and referenced an AR Invoice, This IT will not be displayed on the Document Tree, instead, you'll have to select "Marketing Documents: Referenced Documents" to display it on the relationship map.

If this answer resolves your question, kindly consider marking it as accepted. This helps our community by highlighting useful solutions. Thank you!

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: SLD Data Supplier to to SAP's Support Backbone, for AS Java, through a Web Prox
entry.content:
Good Morning,

If you migrate to SAP Cloud ALM, you need to configure your On Premise server to SLD Data Supplier to Directly Send Data to SAP's Support Backbone because you can not send it to Solman LMDB anymore.

Anyone who migrate from Solman to SAP Cloud ALM has to perform SLD Data Supplier changes.

As my question concerns the migration to SAP Cloud ALM, I have used the Tag SAP Cloud ALM.

Have a nice day.

entry.title: Re: Change data access language in a story
entry.content:
You may try to add ?lang=xx into your story URL, for example: 

https://<TENANT>/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/<TENANT_ID>/bo/story/<STORY_ID>?lang=en

 With this, the sysytem language and data access language is changed, and it's easy to change back and forth as well. 

Check more on the URL parameter: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/a4406994704e4af5a8559a640b496468/fe8d560fce4f4fdd8445455f8effd3c0.html

entry.title: The SAP S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on RISE & SAP BTP Toolbox for SAP Technology Architects 🧰
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on RISE & SAP BTP: Toolbox for SAP Technology Architects 🧰

Back in 2013 when I was studying for SAP HANA Certification I published the following blog, as a central point of reference for SAP HANA technical information, it helped me with what I was studying and helped others with having piles of very useful information in one place:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/the-sap-hana-reference-for-sap-basis-administrators/ba-p/13007539

Fast forward 12 years, and we are now in the same situation with SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP, there is useful documentation all over the place and it would help if there was a central location for SAP Technology Architects to use as a "Toolbox". It will certainly help me 🙂

Implementing and Migrating to and Running S/4 PCE on RISE for the last two years, and as usual collecting a mountain of the most useful documentation as a personal toolbox, it's been clear to see that similar to the situation back in the earlier period of HANA, there is useful RISE documentation in a number of locations, this blog will act as a central resource pointing to all of the locations of the SAP S/4 PCE RISE documentation.

And similarly for the BTP, having been doing BTP (or as it was the SAP Cloud Platform) for 7 years, this blog will be a central location pointing to the most useful BTP documentation for SAP Technology Architects.

The two subjects are combined, SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE with BTP, because in my eyes the two go hand in hand, S/4HANA PCE on RISE is the next generation of the previous On-Premise ERP offerings from SAP, and the BTP contains the next generation of Applications which we will all use with the S/4HANA PCE on RISE, including for example, and not limited to, Integration Suite and CPI & EM/AEM & APIM, obviously CALM as this is replacing in many but not all cases what we do with SolMan, and all of the other Services which we will need pretty much day by day to run S/4HANA PCE on RISE.

The list is divided in to two halves, the SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE, and the SAP BTP. The goal is to have some kind of logic to the list and group and classify related documentation together, instead of a pile of random links.

If you have links to useful documentation which you think will help others and should be added to the list, then please just write it in the comments and I will add it to the list.

Please expect to find changes and new information here, because it will be a live work in progress, it's going to take a few weeks to list and classify everything that I already have.

Tip: search the list for what you are looking for, eg FUE Licensing details

Here we go....

Section 1] General Introduction Documents and RISE Getting Started Guides

SAP Activate Methodology for SAP S/4HANA Transformation

https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/AAE80671-5087-430B-9AA7-8FBE881CF548/phaseAccelerator/S4HANATRANSONPRE

https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools/landscape-management-process.html?anchorId=section_1465999275

SAP S/4 Transformation Readiness Homepage

https://me.sap.com/readinesscheck/home

RISE OnBoarding Resource Center

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise/rise-private.html

RISE OnBoarding Handbook Page

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise.html

RISE with SAP Transition Workbench

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/940e4b48bd434bcb95db32940d08694f/cc75825f2d7049db96b6c60ec269aac6.html

Activate Roadmap Viewer:

https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/

Click on

"RISE with SAP Clean Core Success Plan" and it takes you to

"SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition"

https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapContentPage/a11ec6d657054bf9:t23

RISE PCE ECS Services

https://www.sap.com/about/agreements/policies/hec-services.html?sort=latest_desc&search=private%20edition%20roles%20responsibilities

SAP Help Portal Welcome RISE PCE

https://help.sap.com/doc/daa7b7ca5dfb4fc28a69c3bbd4e22f60/ALL/en-US/SAP_Help_Portal_Welcome_PCE.pdf

S/4 PCE Support Portal

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise/rise-private.html

Onboarding Resource Center

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/sap-customer-onboarding-webinars/sap-s-4hana-customer-onboarding-webinars.html

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise/rise-private.html

SAP RISE Partner Finder

https://partnerfinder.sap.com/

S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Including Transition Paths Architectural Landscape Offering Comparison

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD_PE/b89b8b9026e1456bb2a1df7c0d59c937/f70e688a7cf54c1a8980cc3298b57e30.html

RISE OnBoarding Handbook

https://d.dam.sap.com/a/aW26pWQ/Onboarding%20Handbook.pdf?rc=10

RISE Resource Center

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise.html

RISE PCE Help Page

https://help.sap.com/docs/RISE_WITH_SAP_PRIVATE_CLOUD_EDITION

RISE with SAP OnBoarding Center

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise.html

RISE Private Edition Intro Page

https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/rise/rise-private.html

Webinars

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto  inc, an startup which is making Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto dot io's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto dot io is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.

All of this makes atkrypto dot io the DePIN Decentralised Physical Infrastructure Network solution for Enterprise.

i am currently running into a very strange problem and i cannot find a solution. I have created a analytical query based on I_ActualPlanJournalEntryItem and i created a Parameter for the user to select the hierarchy of GLAccount. This Parameter should select both the hierarchy that is used for the Filter on GLAccount and also for the display in the table. This works with some hierarchies but unfortuneately not for the hierarchies that our users have created in the App F2918 (Manage Global Hierarchies). Dominik

entry.title: Re: HTTP header issue with S4 API call
entry.content:
Hi Navneet,

It might have been a long time since this issue occurred, and perhaps it was resolved in some way. However, I would like this solution to remain here in case others encounter the same problem. I faced a similar issue as well. I encountered this error in an S/4 Public Cloud system with a standard SAP service. I tried removing the headers, but it didn’t work. I even opened a ticket with SAP support. Eventually, I decided to switch the HTTP protocol I was using to OData, and the problem was resolved.

When using OData, it accepts the data in XML format instead of JSON. If you don't want to use OData, you can split the data item by item and then merge it afterward.

entry.title: Tokenization and chain of custody accounting to transition to certified sustainable materials
entry.content:
The business world is exponentially shifting from linear businesses to circular businesses and at the same time, there is a great emphasis on tracking and tracing of raw materials in the value chain and tagging them back to the source such that, there is visibility into various sustainability aspects like sustainable sourcing practices and product carbon footprints. Apart from the voluntary drive from the businesses, the regulations, for example, EU Deforestation Regulations (companies need to demonstrate that the products are deforestation free) for the companies operating within EU, has brought up keen interest from the investors and the business stakeholders such that businesses are compelled to take necessary actions to be complaint towards the regulations and at the same time attracting investors by getting into limelight for taking such sustainability actions.

Along with tracking and tracing, for industries like metals and mining, and chemicals, chain of custody of the raw materials has been a greater hurdle to achieve the required visibility into production processes and tracing and analyzing the product sources and footprints.

                             The need for transparency and traceability in Supply Chains

To tackle these challenges, SAP has come up with a solution called Green Token, which is a chain of custody accounting tool, which emphasizes on mass balancing and segregated accounting. Green Token helps the customers in tracing and tracking back the raw materials and analyzing the mass balances of the raw materials with the help of Tokenization. Tokenization simply refers as the creation of Tokens whenever any circular/recycled content enters the production process and transfer of those tokens across the production processes whenever the recycled content gets transferred/transformed from one process to another.    

TCS team has envisaged a use case for metal and mining industry (Aluminum) on how SAP Green Token helps in mitigating the challenges pertaining to the industry. To depict this, TCS has taken an end-to-end value chain of aluminum production and carved out the phases at which Tokenization is likely to take place and how the tokens are transferred across the value chain.

                                                    Use Case: Green Token for Aluminum Industry

  1. Initiation of Green Tokens (creation of tokens): At this stage, the recycled content from the recycler enters the production process and to record this transaction there is creation of tokens according to the volume of recycled content entering the process.
  2. Material mass balance: Across the production processes, the mass balance is maintained through transfer of tokens and at each phase the volumes are recorded such that there are no discrepancies.
  3. Final product with Green Tokens: The final product would have the total details about the virgin and recycled content within the form of tokens, which would help the manufacturer to get certified and various material certification agencies.

Apart from metal industry, this additional information would help various industries in better understanding their products’ footprint and would help them in identifying and mitigating any risks pertaining to the auditability of the products origin. The segregated accounting would help manufacturers in enabling the transparency required for the materials which are under the regulations radar across the supply chains. Additional information about the product help customers in choosing the right product and in return increases brand value and customer trust towards the product.

Conclusion

As manufacturers are gearing up to tackle these challenges, TCS, which is a strategic partner of SAP, could help them in tackle these challenges with the help of SAP Green Token. TCS, which is a technology consulting firm, would help manufacturers in setting up the systems, enabling the required data transfers into the systems through data genealogies. Apart from that, TCS team, which has rich experience in sustainability strategy consulting would help the manufacturers in pinpointing the various pain areas and solving out through required use cases and technology enablement.

entry.title: Get all operations with final confirmation from AFRU
entry.content:
Hi,

We try to find all operations that are tagged with a final confirmation in EAM app. We have data from AFRU. In this table, for same operations, there are multiple records with different RMHL, the records with maxium RMZHL is the latest one? but it might be either canceled or reversed. 

If we need to find the current final confirmation displayed in EAM, is the one with maxium RMZHL, excuding Reversed records (STOKZ = 'X') and Canceled records (STZHL >=1), noticed STZHL has many values, Null, 0, 1, 2, 3,...? the valid records is WHERE STOKZ <> 'X'  AND  (STZHL < 1)? Then final confirmation from the one with max RMZH? if the value is 'X', it is tagged with final confirmation in EAM workorder app?

e.g,

WITH FinalConfirmation AS (
SELECT
AFRU.* -- Include all AFRU fields
FROM AFRU
INNER JOIN (
SELECT
AUFPL, -- Operation plan number
APLZL, -- Sub-operation number
MAX(RMZHL) AS MaxCounter_RMZHL -- Maximum confirmation counter
FROM AFRU
WHERE STOKZ <> 'X' -- Exclude reversed confirmations
AND  (STZHL < 1)-- Exclude canceled confirmations
GROUP BY AUFPL, APLZL
) MaxRMZHL
ON AFRU.AUFPL = MaxRMZHL.AUFPL
AND AFRU.APLZL = MaxRMZHL.APLZL
AND AFRU.RMZHL = MaxRMZHL.MaxCounter_RMZHL
WHERE AFRU.AUERU = 'X' -- Only include final confirmations
);

Also, I’m wondering when operations will be added to the AFRU table. Some operations appear in the table with Processing% = 0, but others are not included at all? 

SAP EAM Work Order

entry.title: CMIS -> GOS/DMS are in KPro not in CMIS-Business-Objekt-Folder
entry.content:
Hello,

i configured via DMS integration option a new CMIS repository. See article:

Demystifying Document Management Service on BTP - SAP Community

After adding a new attachement to a Product "208270", the file is saved this succes to hte correct folder patch

/Business Object Types/Product/208270

If I did the same based on a SalesOrder or a DIR the files is still in the KPro area.

DIR -> /Knowledge Provider/DMS_PCD1

SalesOrder -> /Knowledge Provider/SOFFPHIO

In following to blog post describes that the "Knowlegde Provider" path is only temporar.

Document Management Integration API | SAP Help Portal

"In DMS, when you create a document info record, a corresponding CMIS folder gets created in the repository. When you add files in document info records, CMIS documents are initially created in the KPro folder (DMS_PCD1). Eventually these documents get moved to the corresponding CMIS business object folder ‘DocumentInfoRecord’."

Second one:

SAP CMIS Profile | SAP Help Portal

  • Knowledge Provider (KPro):

    • The KnowledgeProvider folder is placed under the root node. The folders inside the KPro folder hold the files until they are moved to their corresponding business object type folders.

    • The draft attachments, irrespective of whether they are DMS or GOS based, are first stored in the Knowledge Provider folder. On saving, the attachments are moved to the respective folders under the Business Object Types folder depending on whether they are DMS or GOS based attachments.

      For example, files for DMS and GOS will be created under the PhioClass DMS_PCD1 and SOFFPHIO folders respectively under the main Knowledge Provider folder."

For me following is not clear:

- Create attachment to a Product the file is saved in the correct area "/Business Object Types/Product/208270" and for the other Business objects like SalesOrder and DIR it still stay in the "Knowledge Provider" area.

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Re: Inquiry About Default Sorting Order for accounts.sear...
entry.content:
@MA57 wrote:

I hope this message finds you well Partner Hours Starbucks

I am currently working with the accounts.search API in SAP Customer Data Cloud, and I would like to inquire about the default sorting order of the results when the order by clause is not specified. Could you please clarify what the default order is, and whether it is consistent across different implementations?

Hi there!
Thankyou for reaching out. When the order by clause is not specified in the accounts.search API of SAP Customer Data Cloud, the results are typically returned in the order they are stored in the database, which may not be consistent across different implementations or environments.
To ensure consistent and predictable results, it's best to always specify an order by clause in your queries.

Does this help clarify things for you?

entry.title: From carbon counting to carbon accounting – end-to-end carbon management with SAP
entry.content: Join our virtual live session to explore how SAP Green Ledger is revolutionizing sustainability reporting and management by combining financial and carbon accounting at the business transaction level with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. With this ERP-centric approach to carbon accounting, SAP Green Ledger not only makes carbon traceable and auditable but goes beyond compliance and provides environmental impact alongside financial impact data for strategic and operational decision making at the point of time when business decisions are taken. Don't miss this opportunity to learn about the future of sustainable business!

entry.title: Re: Both "Inactive" and "Active" are set to "No"; set at...
entry.content:
You must first find the business partners where both fields ("Inactive" and "Active") were set to "No":

SELECT "CardCode" FROM OCRD WHERE "validFor"='N' AND "frozenFor"= 'N'

Then you have to set the "Active" field (OCRD.validFor) to YES for all these GPs (via DTW import or manually in the SAP BO client (first remove the Active check mark and then set it again)). 

You can then update the email address via DTW.

entry.title: Re: Getting Timeout Error when adding a S4 HANA system in...
entry.content:
"no connect of TP sapdp01" -- is this is true, then that would mean that your SAP system is in deep trouble?! The dispatcher is not running. But then nobody would be able to log on to that system! Can you log on successfully with SAPGUI using the same logon parameters as you entered in AiE?

But more likely I would guess, that there is no SAP system running on 10.53.252.5, only a standalone gateway. In that case, you would need to tell the gateway, where the real SAP system (the application servers) are running, e.g.

GWHOST=10.53.252.5, ASHOST=<IP of app-server you want to logon to>

You will have to check this with your SAP system administrators.

entry.title: Re: remote enabled function module to read material configuration on plant level
entry.content:
Hello Holger,

you are asking the wrong people... We from NW RFC SDK are responsible for the technical aspects of the RFC communication protocol, but we do not know all of the  probably millions of remote-enabled function modules, that exist in an SAP System...!

You should tag your question with the tag of the application, you want to use here (Material Management?). Then experts from that application area will notice the question, and perhaps they know, which FM you could use.

Best Regards, Ulrich

entry.title: S/4HANA for Product Compliance - labelling follow up questions
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Following up from the detailed question and answers from this post, I have additional questions

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/s-4-product-compliance-label-management/qaq-p/13859930

- Can this standard product compliance module support labels that are not necessarily related to hazards? My customer will have various labels related to the product, which includes hazardous shipping labels, but also logistic labels that contains the batch number and barcode for example. Is it technically possible to assign these labels as a category so that all labels - hazardous and non-hazardous - can be accessed in one same app? 

- Am I right in assuming that as standard there is only one Product stewardship role that is required in order to access the labels? we will have logistics members who need to select labels to print. If not available as standard then I assume we will need to develop custom roles?

- How is information such as bar codes supported? Is this taken elsewhere in SAP as standard?
